thread_tweets.text: They weren’t part of my workflow. My job previously only allowed Github Copilot which didn’t have the Chinese LLMs. To use them I’d have had to connect to their APIs with a different workflow myself. In terms of chat bots, the US ones offer better UIs and integration with storage. Hence more effort. In any event, I’ll likely be trying them more going forward to get a better feel for them but testing models isn’t really a main part of my research.

tweets.text:
Just saw the news that @stripe, @Visa, @Mastercard, and @coinbase are reportedly forming a consortium to launch their own stablecoin

Circle issues USDC, but Coinbase is the reason it got so massive. Coinbase and its users hold around $19 billion of it, more than a quarter of all the USDC in circulation.

And that reach isn't free.

@circle earns money from the interest on the reserves backing USDC, and Coinbase already captures the bigger share by keeping 100% of the interest on USDC held on its platform, while splitting the rest 50/50.

So picture Coinbase with its own coin.

They can point users to it instead of USDC and keep all the interest, instead of splitting it with Circle. It controls the distribution, so nothing stops it.

Circle's biggest partner turning into its biggest competitor could be the story of the century.

So picture Coinbase with its own coin.

Circle's biggest partner turning into its biggest competitor could be the story of the century.

tweets.text:
We’ve just finished a whitehat operation on an exploit discovered in Flooring Protocol.

Now safely in the custody of Yuga Labs:

29 bored apes
4 mutant apes
1 bakc
2 cryptopunks
1 azuki
2 elementals
26 captains
1 moonbird
2 doodles

@0xQuit, our VP of Blockchain recovered the NFTs.  Will leave the details for him to go over in a separate thread.

There was an exploit earlier this morning on the same protocol which had left some collections already raided.  Huge shout to @coffeedev who found that by tweaking the same exploit, there was an even larger risk to other Flooring collections like BAYC and Cryptopunks.

I quietly instructed our GrailsOTC trading desk to front the money and NFTs to rescue the at-risk assets from the protocol.

We will work together with the protocol devs to return these assets once a solution is sorted. Could mean contract relaunches and token reassurances within that protocol - or more.

But thanks to this move we were able to save dozens of assets from impacting the market and flooring protocol tokens from being compromised.

bored ape yacht club

thread_tweets.text: Here is @0xQuit’s breakdown
thread_tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
A Flooring exploit today turned a dust amount of WETH into a near-infinite fpToken balance, allowing the attacker to drain Flooring pools.

This led to a followup opportunist scooping up tokens from the now depleted pools and exchanging them for underlying NFTs.

1/🧵
thread_tweets.full_text: Here is @0xQuit’s breakdown

thread_tweets.text: @0xFreeLunch @0xQuit Happy to help - I won’t forget your strong resolve when you were buying all the apes to start the protocol. We’ll be here when you’re ready.
thread_tweets.full_text: @0xFreeLunch @0xQuit Happy to help - I won’t forget your strong resolve when you were buying all the apes to start the protocol. We’ll be here when you’re ready.

thread_tweets.text: A word from Flooring Protocol’s @0xFreeLunch
thread_tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
1/
Today, an exploit occurred affecting FloorProtocol V2 and BitmapPunks.

Both projects share a similar core contract structure: all fungible tokens issued are pegged 1:1 to the NFTs locked in the contract, allowing users to convert back and forth freely.
thread_tweets.full_text: A word from Flooring Protocol’s @0xFreeLunch

tweets.full_text:
We’ve just finished a whitehat operation on an exploit discovered in Flooring Protocol.

Now safely in the custody of Yuga Labs:

29 bored apes
4 mutant apes
1 bakc
2 cryptopunks
1 azuki
2 elementals
26 captains
1 moonbird
2 doodles

@0xQuit, our VP of Blockchain recovered the NFTs.  Will leave the details for him to go over in a separate thread.

There was an exploit earlier this morning on the same protocol which had left some collections already raided.  Huge shout to @coffeedev who found that by tweaking the same exploit, there was an even larger risk to other Flooring collections like BAYC and Cryptopunks.

I quietly instructed our GrailsOTC trading desk to front the money and NFTs to rescue the at-risk assets from the protocol.

We will work together with the protocol devs to return these assets once a solution is sorted. Could mean contract relaunches and token reassurances within that protocol - or more.

But thanks to this move we were able to save dozens of assets from impacting the market and flooring protocol tokens from being compromised.

tweets.text:
$ZEC took an exploit, flash crashed to $250, and is already bouncing back hard after devs dropped the Ironwood proposal to move holders to a new verified pool.

Once again, massive drama turned into a massive opportunity.

That kind of resilience tells you everything you need to know about how badly the market wants privacy. Maybe it really is the last 1000x left out there.

Arcium TGE this month 👀

tweets.text: One heuristic for making startups AI-proof is to make things human intelligence can't compete with either. For example, marketplaces are proof against AI competitors for the same reason they're proof against human ones.

thread_tweets.text:
Patents are similarly protective. It's no use being able to think of an idea if you can't legally implement it.

(AIs might of course be better at finding ways around patents. But on the other hand patent filers can also use AIs to predict these and seal them off.)

tweets.text: @pmarca Any founder who has begun by making something they themselves wanted will be better at convincing users than investors. Imagine Woz demoing the Apple I to the Homebrew Computer Club or Larry Page demoing BackRub to fellow students vs either pitching a VC.

thread_tweets.text: VCs shouldn't have to represent the audience. A founder should be able to explain his or her startup to someone who's not a potential user. My point is just that this takes more effort.
thread_tweets.full_text: VCs shouldn't have to represent the audience. A founder should be able to explain his or her startup to someone who's not a potential user. My point is just that this takes more effort.

thread_tweets.text: @kodykendall @pmarca Thanks, but I consider VCs as partners, not enemies. There are some bad ones, as there are in any industry, but there are some really good ones too.
